title: Figure 1 from: Ivanov H, Linev A, Pochileev I, Stoyanova V (2026) Droplet digital PCR outperforms qPCR by revealing clinically relevant low-frequency KRAS mutations in colorectal cancer. Pharmacia 73: 1-7. https://doi.org/10.3897/pharmacia.73.e187140
author: Ivanov, Hristo
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Figure 1 Confusion matrix comparing qPCR and ddPCR results for KRAS mutation detection in 50 FFPECRC samples. ddPCR detected all qPCR-positive cases and identified six additional low-VAF mutations not detected by qPCR. This demonstrates that ddPCR achieves perfect sensitivity relative to qPCR while identifying additional mutation-positive cases, underscoring its improved ability to capture low-frequency variants.
steps_raw: Figure 1 Confusion matrix comparing qPCR and ddPCR results for KRAS mutation detection in 50 FFPECRC samples. ddPCR detected all qPCR-positive cases and identified six additional low-VAF mutations not detected by qPCR. This demonstrates that ddPCR achieves perfect sensitivity relative to qPCR while identifying additional mutation-positive cases, underscoring its improved ability to capture low-frequency variants.
